page: Vultr vbm-112c-2048gb-8-a100-gpu is a bare metal server configured with dual Intel Xeon 8480+ processors, delivering 112 physical cores and 224 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 2048.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 9.14 GB of RAM per physical core to support memory-intensive operations.

This server features eight integrated NVIDIA A100 GPUs from the Ampere architecture, providing a combined total of 640 GB of high-bandwidth VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 3840 GB NVMe SSD, ensuring high-speed local data access. The configuration also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Given the bare metal architecture and the density of high-end accelerators, this server offers high resource density for enterprise-scale workloads. It is designed for intensive parallel computing tasks, including large-scale machine learning model training, deep learning inference, high-performance computing simulations, and large-scale data analytics.
description: Vultr vbm-112c-2048gb-8-a100-gpu is a high-density bare metal server designed for parallel processing and accelerator-heavy workloads. It features two Intel Xeon 8480+ processors with 112 physical cores and 224 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 2048.0 GB of system memory. The primary hardware highlight is the inclusion of eight NVIDIA A100 GPUs, delivering a total of 640 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 3840 GB NVMe SSD, and the system includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. This bare metal configuration provides dedicated hardware resources without hypervisor overhead, making it suitable for large-scale machine learning training, deep learning inference, and high-performance computing workloads.
og_description: Vultr vbm-112c-2048gb-8-a100-gpu is a bare metal server featuring 224 vCPUs, 2048 GB RAM, a 3840 GB NVMe SSD, and eight NVIDIA A100 GPUs with 640 GB of total VRAM for intensive parallel workloads.

tagline: A bare metal accelerator platform featuring eight NVIDIA A100 GPUs and 2048 GB RAM for intensive parallel computing workloads.
